behavior_summary: CANDLBRA::look: When the player 'looks at' the candelabra the item is prepared for a short info/action and Item::bark is invoked with the literal string "candelabra ", causing the object to present that description to the player.
CANDLBRA::anim: An animation/auxiliary process is spawned (METHOD::0610) for the candelabra; this is likely used to drive a visual or timed effect associated with the object.

CANDLBRA::use: The item's frame value is read (Item::getFrame) and the low two bits are tested. If both low bits are clear the frame is incremented by one; otherwise the low two bits are cleared (frame & 0xFC). The modified frame is written back with Item::setFrame, effectively toggling a visual/state bit (e.g., lit/unlit or on/off).
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: each exposed routine is an event handler (U8P_CANDLBRA::look/anim/use) invoked by the engine as a 'process'.
Imply/suspend pattern: after external calls (Item::bark, spawn) the code uses an implies/pop and suspend-style control sequence (engine pattern for waiting or chaining effects).
Branch in U8P_CANDLBRA::use based on (frame & 3) == 0 controls two distinct write-backs to Item::setFrame (increment vs. clear low bits).
U8P_CANDLBRA::anim uses a spawn call to METHOD::0610 rather than modifying frame directly, separating animation from state changes.
open_questions: What exact in-game visual or gameplay meaning do the low two frame bits encode (literal lit/unlit, level of flame, or other visual variants)? (inferred, confidence: medium)
What specific behavior METHOD::0610 implements when spawned for the candelabra (animation, timed toggle, particle effect)? (inferred, confidence: low)
Are there any global flags elsewhere that interact with the candelabra's state transitions (none are written or read here, but global interactions are possible)? (confidence: low)
Is the 'anim' event invoked automatically on use/other events or only by external triggers (entry conditions for anim not present here)? (confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive bark for the candelabra when the player examines it (display the string "candelabra ") and perform standard item info/exclude setup for the call.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_CANDLBRA::look) — invoked by the engine when an actor/player examines/looks at the candelabra.

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_CANDLBRA::look' (examine) event: prepare item context and call Item::bark with the literal description.

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::anim
function_map.role: Spawn an auxiliary animation/process (METHOD::0610) for visual or timed effects tied to the candelabra.

function_map.name: U8P_CANDLBRA::use
function_map.role: Toggle or update the candelabra's visual state by reading and writing the item's frame low bits via Item::getFrame/Item::setFrame.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided routine used to show short item text/messages; called by U8P_CANDLBRA::look to display 'candelabra '.

function_map.name: METHOD::0610
function_map.role: External method identifier used with spawn to run an animation/auxiliary process for the item.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Read the item's uword frame/state; used by use to inspect low bits.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Write a uword frame/state back to the item; used by use to apply toggled state.

external_calls.purpose: Display the fixed description string 'candelabra ' to the inspecting process/player.
Spawn an animation/auxiliary method/process to manage visual or timed effects for the candelabra.
Retrieve the current frame/uword that encodes the item's visual/state bits for decision making.
Write back a modified frame value either incremented by one or with the low two bits cleared to effect a state change.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: CANDLBRA::look: When the player 'looks at' the candelabra the item is prepared for a short info/action and Item::bark is invoked with the literal string "candelabra ", causing the object to present that description to the player.
